title: frangula californica subsp. ursina
intro: Frangula californica subsp. ursina, also known as bearberry, is a species of shrub in the coffee family (Rubiaceae) native to the western regions of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and have anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Frangula californica subsp. ursina include treating urinary tract infections and digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea. Herbal preparations of bearberry can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are often used to support kidney and bladder health.
